title: Cheesy Chorizo High Protein Flatbreads
summary: These fluffy flatbreads give the ultimate cheese pull. Plus with high protein dough and chorizo they're a perfect way to boost your protein while still feeling indulgent.

method.description: Finely chop the chorizo, onion, garlic and red chilli.

Add the chorizo to a frying pan with a splash of olive oil and set over a medium heat. Cook until some of the fat has rendered out and the chorizo is beginning to crisp, around 3 mins, then add the onion and cook until softened, for 10 mins.
Add the garlic, red chilli, smoked paprika and ground cumin and cook for 1 minute. Tip into a bowl.
Drain the black beans then add them to the bowl, add the cheese. Finely chop the fresh coriander, then add. Stir to combine and adjust the salt level to your liking. Set aside.

Now make the dough. Add the white bread flour, wholemeal bread flour, Greek yoghurt, baking powder and salt to a large bowl. Mix with a spoon until it forms a dough, then transfer to a lightly floured surface and knead for a minute until it comes together. (Or, if using a stand mixer, add all of the ingredients to the bowl and mix with a dough hook for a minute.)
Cut the dough into equal pieces (if you're making the recipe to serve 6, cut it into 6 pieces and so on). Flour the surface again, then roll each piece into a ball.

Flatten each ball so it makes a 15 - 20cm disk, then add a big handful of filling to the middle, leaving a large rim, then fold the outsides over the filling to seal them inside the flatbread. Flatten into a chunky disk.

Heat a large non-stick frying pan over a medium head. Add a small splash of olive oil. Add the flatbreads, one at a time, and cook for 5 mins on each side, until the bread is gold and puffed up. Set aside and repeat with the rest. Slice and serve immediately, or wrap and freezer to eat later.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
